payload.title: Client workspaces should host Narada Sites under .narada by default
payload.summary: The Staccato setup exposed a Site-root placement issue. We initially placed Narada governance files at the root of a OneDrive client business folder. That made the folder visually and semantically sloppy because the folder already had a native identity as a client workspace containing agreements, PBIX files, and business artifacts. We migrated the Narada Site embodiment into a hidden .narada subfolder and kept the workspace root clean.
evidence: Before correction, AGENTS.md, README.md, config.json, kb, inbox, tasks, chapters, decisions, observations, friction, and requests lived beside client artifacts in the Staccato OneDrive folder. After correction, the workspace root contains .git, .gitignore, .narada, agreements, Staccato 2011 LLC NDA.docx, and staccato-model.pbix. The client-service Site root is now the .narada folder, while the parent remains the client workspace. Reciprocal data and ELT Site configs were updated to target the .narada Site path.
proposal: Add doctrine and/or bootstrap guidance: a Site may inhabit a workspace without being identical to the workspace. For client/business/document folders, Narada should default to a .narada subfolder unless the operator explicitly chooses root-level Site materialization. Absorption should target Site roots, not ambient workspace folders, and should preserve the distinction between workspace, Site embodiment, and authority locus.
payload.recommendation: Update Site bootstrap/doctor guidance to warn when governance files are placed at the root of a client/business workspace containing non-Narada artifacts. Prefer workspace_root plus site_root fields in config for inhabited workspaces. Treat client/business artifacts as outside Narada unless explicitly admitted.

promotion.note: Envelope archived; no target-zone mutation was performed.
